Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) regulations govern commercial trucking, and critical evidence — including black box data, driver logs, and inspection records — can be lost or destroyed without a proper spoliation hold in place.

At WreckMatch, we've built a network of 800+ participating truck accident attorneys across Texas and nationwide who understand exactly how to handle the complexities of commercial vehicle litigation. Get matched with a Corpus Christi truck accident attorney today: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form

reddit_title: Injured in an 18-wheeler crash in Corpus Christi, TX — here's what you need to know about protecting your claim
reddit_body:
If you were recently involved in an 18-wheeler crash in Corpus Christi, the single most important thing to understand is that evidence disappears fast. Commercial trucks are equipped with black boxes (EDRs) that record speed, braking, and driver behavior — but trucking companies are not always required to preserve that data indefinitely. Sending a spoliation letter through an attorney as quickly as possible can be the difference between having key evidence and losing it forever.

Federal FMCSA regulations add a layer of complexity that most general personal injury attorneys aren't fully equipped to handle. Driver logs, hours-of-service violations, maintenance records, and carrier insurance requirements all factor into truck accident claims. Texas also has specific statutes of limitations and comparative fault rules that can affect your recovery, so acting quickly matters both legally and practically.

For anyone who isn't sure where to start, wreckmatch.com is a free service that matches truck accident victims with attorneys who specifically handle commercial vehicle cases. It's not a law firm — it's a matching platform connected to 800+ participating attorneys, so you can find someone with the right experience for your situation without having to cold-call a dozen law offices on your own.
